title: oligactis coriacea
intro: Oligactis coriacea, also known as the rough passion flower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Oligactis coriacea include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and treat various skin conditions. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and optimal preparations.
